title: Make dim_country construction deterministic — duplicate iso3 rows (Turkey et al.) resolved by arbitrary dropDuplicates, breaking alias resolution run-to-run
description: missing_countries hardcodes Turkey/TUR (plus Syria, Libya, North Korea, etc.) with the comment 'appear in procurement/supply data but not in EPI/WB' — but EPI 2024 DOES include Turkey (as 'Turkiye'/'Türkiye'; the notebook's own alias table expects that spelling to arrive from EPI). Both rows hash to the same country_key (key = f(iso3) via generate_country_key), and dropDuplicates(['country_key']) keeps an ARBITRARY row. If the EPI-named row ('Türkiye') wins, every alias targeting standard name 'Turkey' is silently dropped by the inner join when building country_lookup, and procurement rows saying 'Turkey' fall to Unknown-Global. Which behavior you get can flip between runs — nondeterministic country naming and mapping coverage that later looks like phantom data drift.
acceptanceCriteria: dim_country resolution is deterministic: duplicate iso3 across sources resolved by explicit precedence (e.g. curated missing_countries names win over source names, or vice versa — decided and commented in the notebook), implemented via row_number() over a precedence column, not bare dropDuplicates.

missing_countries no longer double-registers countries present in EPI: either remove entries EPI already covers (verify per-entry against silver_epi2024results iso values) or rely on the precedence rule to make the overlap harmless — but the misleading 'not in EPI/WB' comment (silver-to-gold2:257-258) is corrected either way.

Alias closure check passes after /run-gold: every standard_name in country_aliases_with_confidence exists as a country_name_std in gold_dim_country (query returning 0 missing rows). Today 'Turkey' aliases can vanish silently via the inner join at lines 481-483.
Two consecutive /run-gold executions produce identical gold_dim_country content (deterministic — verify by EXCEPT/checksum comparison).
[Erik/Fabric] Spot-check in the report: procurement rows with country 'Turkey' (and 'Türkiye' encoding variants) map to the Turkey dim row, not Unknown - Global.
notes: AUDIT-DERIVED (pipeline quality audit 2026-07-13): finding #5 (critical tier). Not spec-decomposed — empty drift fingerprints.

EVIDENCE: missing_countries seed at silver-to-gold2 notebook-content.py:259-268 (includes ('Turkey','TUR',...)); country_key = generate_country_key over iso3 at lines 231-235 and 460; dropDuplicates(['country_key']) at line 465; alias inner join that silently drops orphaned aliases at lines 481-483; 'Türkiye' -> 'Turkey' aliases at lines 175-180 prove EPI-side Turkey naming is expected.
WHY NONDETERMINISTIC: Spark dropDuplicates keeps an arbitrary row per key (partition/order dependent) — the winner can change between runs/cluster configs. The bug is invisible on any single run.

SCOPE CHECK: audit only verified Turkey as a confirmed overlap; sweep the other missing_countries entries (PRK, YEM, SYR, LBY, XKX, SMR, NRU) against actual EPI iso values as part of the fix — some are genuinely absent from EPI and should stay.
COORDINATE with task-023 (same file, same alias/lookup machinery): whichever name wins precedence determines which alias rows survive; run task-023's acceptance queries again after this lands.

CLAUDE HALF DONE 2026-07-14: added a source_precedence column to each dim_country union branch (curated missing_countries=1, EPI=2, unknown=3) and replaced dropDuplicates(['country_key']) with row_number() over partitionBy('country_key').orderBy(source_precedence asc, country_name_std asc), keeping rn==1. Helper cols dropped by the existing final .select -> gold_dim_country schema byte-identical. Misleading 'not in EPI/WB' comment corrected. py_compile passes. PRECEDENCE DECISION (commented inline ~L475-491): curated names WIN over EPI spellings, because the alias table resolves variants TO curated spellings ('Türkiye'->'Turkey' etc.), so curated-wins maximises alias closure; country_key identical across colliding rows so fact_epi_score (joined on iso3) is unaffected. Known winner: TUR -> 'Turkey' (not 'Türkiye') -> task-023 keeps all 6 Turkey aliases.

ERIK (Fabric) — remaining acceptance: (crit 4) run /run-gold twice, EXCEPT/checksum-compare gold_dim_country -> identical; (crit 3) run the alias-closure query (every country_aliases standard_name exists as country_name_std) -> 0 missing [curated-collision closure is guaranteed by the fix; residual gaps would be non-curated EPI spellings = task-023 territory]; (crit 5) report spot-check: procurement 'Turkey'/'Türkiye' map to the Turkey dim row, not Unknown-Global.

NON-BLOCKING (noted by implementer): epi.dropDuplicates(['iso3']) ~L242 is the same nondeterminism class but benign today (silver_epi2024results is one-row-per-country); consider an assert if EPI ingestion changes.

MEASURED 2026-07-22: the 47 unmapped-country rows in fact_supply_share resolve to exactly three source spellings — 'Türkiye' (45 rows), 'Cape Verde' (1), 'Brasilia' (1). Three different root causes, not one alias gap:

(a) TÜRKIYE, 45 rows — NOT a missing alias. silver-to-gold2:165-170 already carries SIX variants: 'Türkiye' (0.90 encoding_variant), 'TÃ¼rkiye', 'TÃƒÂ¼rkiye', 'TÃƒÂ¯Â¿Â½rkiye', 'TÃ¯Â¿Â½rkiye' (all 0.80 corrupted_encoding) and 'Turkyie' (0.85 typo). gold_dim_country_lookup holds one row PER ALIAS (see the comment at :1010), so the alias is present in the join target. The join s.country = l.lookup_name therefore fails on a SEVENTH byte-sequence not yet covered — most likely a literal U+FFFD replacement char ('T\uFFFDrkiye') introduced when the Copy activity fetched the CSV over HTTP without an explicit encoding. DO NOT add a guessed variant: get the actual codepoints first (query below). This is the largest single mapping gap and Türkiye is a material supplier of several CRMs (borate, feldspar, chromium), so it matters for task-038's HHI, not just for the DQ check.

(b) CAPE VERDE, 1 row — genuinely absent. grep for 'Verde' across silver-to-gold2 returns ZERO hits, so neither 'Cape Verde' nor 'Cabo Verde' exists in the alias seed or the dim. Needs a new alias pointing at whatever gold_dim_country uses as the standard name.

(c) BRASILIA, 1 row — a SOURCE DATA ERROR, not an alias gap. Brasília is the capital city of Brazil, not a country; the Global CSV has a city in a country column. Recommend aliasing it deliberately with a low confidence and an honest match_type, e.g. ('Brasilia','Brazil',0.60,'source_error'), so the existing confidence/match_type machinery keeps it traceable rather than silently laundering bad source data into a clean country mapping.

IMPACT: fixing all three should take fact_supply_share's duplicated_grains from 2 to 0, which makes grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share pass and unblocks the DQ gate — see task-026 FINDING 2 (corrected). Crit 5 of THIS task (procurement 'Turkey'/'Türkiye' -> Turkey dim row, not Unknown-Global) is the same defect measured from the procurement side.

DIAGNOSTIC to run before adding any Türkiye variant:
  spark.sql("""SELECT country, length(country) AS len FROM oem_lh.silver_globalsupplyshares WHERE country LIKE 'T%rkiye' GROUP BY country, length(country)""").show(10, False)
  # then, on the returned value:
  row = spark.sql("SELECT DISTINCT country FROM oem_lh.silver_globalsupplyshares WHERE country LIKE 'T%rkiye'").collect()
  for r in row: print(repr(r['country']), [hex(ord(c)) for c in r['country']])

ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED IN REPO 2026-07-22 (supersedes the 'add a Türkiye alias' option in the note above — that would have been a symptom fix). The stored value is 'T\uFFFDrkiye': codepoints 0x54, 0xFFFD, 0x72, 0x6B, 0x69, 0x79, 0x65 (measured, not inferred). U+FFFD is the Unicode REPLACEMENT CHARACTER — the original byte is DESTROYED, so no alias can recover the real spelling; it can only pattern-match the damage.

SOURCE EVIDENCE: fetched both CSVs directly from raw.githubusercontent.com and scanned every byte. fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv contains exactly ONE distinct non-ASCII byte, 0xFC, appearing 45 times; fact_EUSupplyShares&t.csv likewise, 15 times. In both files the ONLY affected field value is 'Türkiye'. Neither file decodes as UTF-8 (fails at the first 0xFC); both decode cleanly as windows-1252. 0xFC is 'ü' in Windows-1252/Latin-1; in UTF-8 'ü' would be the two bytes C3 BC, so a lone 0xFC is invalid UTF-8 and the decoder substitutes U+FFFD. The 45 mangled rows match the 45 unmapped Türkiye rows exactly.

WHY IT HAPPENED: neither Copy activity's DelimitedText dataset declared an encoding, so Fabric applied its UTF-8 default to Latin-1 source files. FIX APPLIED to fabric/orchestrator_pipeline_bronze_to_gold.DataPipeline/pipeline-content.json — added "encodingName": "WINDOWS-1252" to the datasetSettings.typeProperties of BOTH bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares and bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares (property name and the value 'WINDOWS-1252' verified against Microsoft's DelimitedText format reference, not guessed). JSON revalidated after the edit.

CONSEQUENCE: once deployed, 'Türkiye' lands intact in bronze and the EXISTING alias at silver-to-gold2:165 ('Türkiye' -> 'Turkey', 0.90, encoding_variant) matches it. NO new alias is needed. This also explains the five pre-existing 'corrupted_encoding' aliases at :166-170 — each is a patch for a different stage of this same unfixed decode, and none of them could ever have matched U+FFFD directly. Consider retiring them once the encoding fix is confirmed, but only after measuring that no other ingestion path still produces them.

REQUIRES: deploy the pipeline to Fabric + re-run. This will CHANGE the verification numbers, favourably — 45 rows move from Unknown-Global to Turkey, unmapped countries drop from 47 to 2 (Cape Verde, Brasilia), and duplicated_grains should fall from 2 toward 0. Re-measure task-026 FINDING 2 after the re-run.

CONFIRMED 2026-07-22 20:01 — the WINDOWS-1252 encoding fix WORKS. After deploy + pipeline re-run, the unmapped-country query returns only 'Cape Verde' (1) and 'Brasilia' (1). Türkiye's 45 rows now decode correctly and are matched by the pre-existing alias at silver-to-gold2:165. Unmapped countries 47 -> 2; country coverage 98.2% -> 99.92%. No new alias was needed and none was added — the root-cause fix in pipeline-content.json (commit fe47b69) was sufficient. NOTE for the earlier stale-measurement trap: the first post-fix query at 19:43 still showed Türkiye because the pipeline had not been re-run (dataflow refresh timestamps were still 18:42); only the 20:01 run reflects the fix.

task_verification.notes: Claude code-half verified (owner:both). dropDuplicates(['country_key']) replaced with row_number() over partitionBy('country_key').orderBy(source_precedence asc, country_name_std asc), rn==1. Precedence real+total+commented (L456-491); curated=1 wins over EPI spellings, independently confirmed correct (alias table maps variants TO curated names, so curated-wins maximises alias closure; TUR->'Turkey' deterministically). Final .select drops helper cols -> gold_dim_country schema byte-identical (7 cols). py_compile passes. runtime_validation=partial: two-run EXCEPT determinism, alias-closure query, Turkey report spot-check are Erik's Fabric half; code makes all three achievable. Surgical enough that task-023 builds on it cleanly.

verification_history.summary: Root-cause deterministic fix (not symptom-hide); precedence total & commented; schema preserved; py_compile OK. Fabric run criteria = Erik.
